title: Evaluation of carcinogenic risks related to nitrate exposure in drinking water in Iran
author: Darvishmotevalli, Moradnia, et al.
materials: the amount of nitrosatable substances ingested,
the rate of in vivo nitrosation and

steps.title: the amount of nitrite ingested or formed from nitrate,
steps.instruction: the amount of nitrite ingested or formed from nitrate,

steps.title: the carcinogenic potential of the resulting nitrosamine [7]
steps.instruction: the carcinogenic potential of the resulting nitrosamine [7]. According to the above, the modelling techniques were computed from outputs of Monte Carlo simulations are represented as following:

description: Nitrate and nitrite are naturally produced by biological oxidation of nitrogen in the soil, plants, water, and also lightning. These pollutants are also generally formed by anthropogenic processes such as agricultural activities (including inorganic chemical fertilizer and organic livestock manures), industrial wastewater discharges, wastewater treatment, and motor vehicles [[1], [2], [3]]. Nowadays, intensive usage of nitrogeneos fertilizers in agriculture and runoff are the major pollution source for drinking water supplies [4]. In this study, Monte Carlo analysis and mathematical models [5,6] were used to estimate cancer risk associated with endogenous nitrosamine formation through drinking water containing nitrate concentrations.
